title: 'If you're not close enough, your photo is not good enough': Remembering Raghu Rai
content: Legendary photographer Raghu Rai, who shaped India's visual memory for over five decades, passed away on Sunday. His iconic images, from the Bhopal Gas tragedy to portraits of national figures, gave permanence to history. Rai believed visual history was more important than fine art, emphasizing closeness to truth in his immersive photographic practice.

title: 'We’ve got some work': Ponting flags concerns despite unbeaten PBKS run
content: Ricky Ponting hailed Shreyas Iyer’s leadership after Punjab Kings pulled off a record chase of 265 against Delhi Capitals, with Iyer anchoring the win with an unbeaten 71. He praised the strong start by Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya, calling their partnership key. Ponting said the team draws confidence from Iyer’s presence in chases, but added that bowling and fielding still need improvement despite the historic victory.

title: 'Shots will be fired': Karoline Leavitt’s pre-dinner comments go viral after White House shooting
content: A shooting scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner saw President Trump evacuated after a suspect armed with multiple weapons breached a security checkpoint.  Secret Service agents engaged the individual, with one officer sustaining non-critical injuries. The suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended. Trump later described the event as an attempted assassination.

title: 'You're not getting a great job right out of college: Amazon CEO's harsh truth for students
content: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy advises young professionals that career growth often begins with smaller roles, emphasizing the importance of starting at the ground level and paying dues. He highlighted continuous learning and reliability as crucial for success, suggesting that exploring different fields can help discover one's passions and navigate the evolving job market.

title: Ashwin rallies behind Nair after costly drops, says 'I’m not making excuses'
content: Punjab Kings achieved a record IPL chase, but Karun Nair's fielding errors proved costly. He dropped Shreyas Iyer twice, crucial misses that allowed Iyer to score a match-winning 71. Despite the blunders, Ravichandran Ashwin defended Nair, suggesting fatigue and a sudden game entry might have contributed to his struggles.

title: TCS Nashik probe runs into hurdles as accused has different passwords for each file and app
content: Police are facing challenges in a sexual assault and religious coercion probe linked to a Nashik TCS office. The prime accused is using multiple passwords and Face ID on his cellphone, hindering access to crucial digital evidence. Forensic experts are assisting investigators to unlock the device, which is key to the ongoing investigation into nine registered cases.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Zoomed camera focusing on legs': Drunk flyer films female crew on Hyderabad–Udaipur flight
content: An IndiGo passenger bound for Udaipur was apprehended at Hyderabad airport for allegedly photographing female cabin crew inappropriately and harassing a co-passenger. The businessman, reportedly intoxicated, was deboarded after ignoring warnings. Police have registered a case under the new penal code for insulting a woman's modesty.

title: ‘I just want to go home’: Tearful Erika Kirk escorted out after White House dinner
content: Erika Kirk was seen being escorted from the White House Correspondents' Dinner in tears after gunfire caused panic and evacuation. The incident evoked sympathy due to her husband Charlie Kirk's death last year. The dinner was disrupted by a shooting near the security screening area, leading to a lockdown and the arrest of a suspect.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints Rs 2.95 cr amid ‘Michael' and ‘Bhooth Bangla’ buzz
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its strong box office run, nearing Rs 1,130 crore in India and Rs 1,780 crore worldwide after 38 days. Despite competition, the film saw significant growth on its 6th Saturday, earning Rs 2.95 crore domestically.  Meanwhile, 'Bhooth Bangla' and 'Michael' also performed on Saturday.

title: Heated! Babar Azam vs umpire steals the show in PSL - Watch
content: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam was seen in a heated exchange with the umpire during a PSL match against Lahore Qalandars. Two separate ball changes were necessitated within the first three overs due to boundaries, leading to Azam's visible frustration. This unusual occurrence early in the innings clearly unsettled the skipper.

title: Chhattisgarh village stays awake to see first light bulb glow after decades of isolation
content: For the first time, the remote village of Irapanar in Abujhmarh has electricity, marking a significant arrival after decades of isolation.  Despite challenging terrain, power company teams worked tirelessly to bring light, enabling children to study, mobile phone use, and the introduction of basic appliances. This electrification promises improved access to education, healthcare, and communication for the community.

title: 'Not democracy, but TMC's reign of thuggery': Rahul Gandhi over killing of Congress worker
content: Rahul Gandhi has strongly condemned the murder of Congress worker Debdeep Chatterjee in West Bengal, attributing the killing to "TMC thugs" following phase-1 polling. He accused the ruling party of replacing democracy with "thuggery" and demanded immediate arrests, harsh punishment, and compensation for the victim's family, vowing to uphold non-violence.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s injury update: Is it serious?
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi suffered an injury scare after a record-breaking century against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The 15-year-old limped off with a suspected hamstring issue, though batting coach Vikram Rathour indicated it was likely severe cramps and not serious. Sooryavanshi had earlier smashed 103 off 37 balls.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sixes set social media on fire — best fan reactions from IPL 2026
content: A 15-year-old Vaibhav Suryavanshi set the internet ablaze with four consecutive sixes and a century in just 36 balls. His explosive batting against Sunrisers Hyderabad captivated fans, sparking widespread awe and comparisons to cricketing legends. Despite his team's loss, Suryavanshi's emotional reaction underscored the profound impact of his arrival on the cricket scene, marking him as a present-day phenomenon.

title: Man found hanging inside women's washroom at Delhi's Inderlok Metro station
content: A man's body was discovered hanging inside a locked women's washroom at Inderlok Metro station on Saturday evening. The discovery was made after a foul smell was reported from the locked toilet. Police believe the deceased, an unidentified man around 40, was likely the washroom caretaker, missing for two days.

title: Banda hottest in country at 47.4℃ as UP reels under heatwave
content: Uttar Pradesh is reeling under a severe heatwave, with Banda recording a scorching 47.4 degrees Celsius, its highest in April since 1951. Many districts exceeded 42 degrees Celsius, and the heatwave is expected to persist until Monday. Relief might arrive Tuesday with a western disturbance bringing light rainfall.

title: Shocking! Fangirl forcibly pulls Abhishek's hand; security steps in - Watch
content: A fan grabbing Abhishek Sharma’s hand during IPL 2026 sparked debate over personal boundaries, with security stepping in as the moment went viral. On the field, he scored 57 alongside Ishan Kishan’s 74 to help Sunrisers Hyderabad chase 229 against Rajasthan Royals. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s brilliant 103 went in vain as SRH sealed a record chase.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: Engine fire on SWISS Delhi-Zurich flight during take off roll; aircraft evacuated on runway
content: Six passengers were hospitalized after a SWISS flight from Delhi to Zurich was evacuated on the runway due to an engine fire during takeoff. The Airbus A330's engine number one failed at 104 knots, prompting the crew to abort the takeoff and evacuate all 232 passengers and crew using emergency slides.

title: Trump evacuated from media dinner after gunshots; supporters say another assassination attempt
content: Shots rang out near the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, prompting the hurried evacuation of President Trump and other dignitaries. The Secret Service apprehended one individual and is investigating the incident, which occurred shortly before Trump was to deliver his keynote speech.

title: Photographer Raghu Rai passes away; his lens captured Indira Gandhi, Dalai Lama, Mother Teresa
content: Renowned Indian photographer Raghu Rai, celebrated for capturing the nation's essence, has passed away at 83. His illustrious career spanned six decades, documenting pivotal moments like the Bangladesh refugee crisis and the Bhopal gas tragedy. Rai's lens also immortalized India's leaders and ordinary citizens, offering profound insights.

title: ‘Lone wolf’: Trump shares visuals of shooter Cole Allen Thomas running past security
content: A gunman, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, attempted to breach security at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, firing at a Secret Service agent who was protected by a bulletproof vest. President Trump described the suspect as a "lone wolf" and stated the attack was not linked to the war in Iran.

title: 'Ginger-garlic can’t be a dish': Bhagwant Mann’s jab at defecting AAP MPs
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann is seeking a meeting with President Murmu to recall seven Rajya Sabha MPs who defected from AAP.  A minister questioned the authenticity of two signatures on the defection letter, suggesting a potential forgery.  The party argues these defections betray the mandate and undermine democracy, seeking a constitutional precedent for removal.

title: Bomb attack kills 14, injures 38 in Colombia weeks before presidential election
content: A deadly highway bomb attack in Colombia's Cauca region has killed 14 and injured 38. The blast occurred as assailants blocked the road. Authorities blame FARC dissidents. This violence escalates security concerns just weeks before the presidential election. The attack follows a wave of incidents targeting public infrastructure across southwestern Colombia.

title: Preity Zinta punches Arya, batter’s reaction goes viral – Watch
content: Punjab Kings pulled off a record chase against Delhi Capitals, but a playful moment stole the show as Preity Zinta jokingly punched Priyansh Arya, whose reaction went viral. Ravi Shastri quipped, “Oh, that was a good punch.” Earlier, KL Rahul’s 152 powered DC to 264/2, but Punjab responded strongly, with Prabhsimran, Arya, and Shreyas Iyer leading a stunning chase to stay unbeaten.

title: AAP implosion: BJP gains Rajya Sabha numbers, but will it be advantage Congress in Punjab?
content: The AAP, as expected, reacted very strongly to this development and accused the BJP of “Operation Lotus”, a term used to define saffron party engineered defections of political rivals. AAP leader Sanjay Singh alleged that central agencies like the Enforcement Directorate (ED) and the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) were misused to pressure and break away AAP leaders. AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal accused the BJP of betraying Punjab, which is ruled by AAP and goes to assembly polls next year.

title: 'Relieved to learn Trump, First Lady safe': PM Modi says violence has no place in democracy
content: Prime Minister Modi expressed relief after a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. President Trump, the First Lady, and Vice President Vance were unharmed. The assailant, armed with a shotgun, was apprehended by the Secret Service after injuring an agent, who was protected by a vest. Panic ensued as guests sought cover.

title: Watch: Donald Trump ducks for cover as security rushes to evacuate him
content: Panic erupted at the White House Correspondents' Dinner as gunshots were heard, forcing President Trump and Vice President Vance to be swiftly evacuated. Secret Service agents secured the venue, apprehending a suspect. Despite the scare, Trump praised security and declared "the show must go on." The incident caused widespread confusion and a massive security response.

title: When Australia starts watching: How Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became IPL 2026's global breakout star
content: Jaipur witnessed a cricketing marvel as 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a 36-ball century, including 12 sixes. Opposing captain Pat Cummins, a seasoned bowler, declared Sooryavanshi his new favourite player, awestruck by his power-hitting.  This performance has captivated the cricket world, with fellow players also acknowledging his exceptional talent and game awareness.

title: UP triple murder: 3 shot dead after cake-smearing dispute at birthday party inside gym
content: A birthday celebration in Bulandshahr tragically turned violent after a minor cake-smearing prank escalated into a fatal altercation. Three young men were shot and killed by the birthday boy and his accomplices, who allegedly returned with firearms after an initial argument. Investigations are ongoing to apprehend the absconding accused.

title: Major change in badminton! BWF approves new 3x15 scoring system
content: The BWF has approved a new 3x15 scoring system in badminton, set to be implemented from January, after strong support at its AGM. While trials showed shorter, more exciting matches, opinions are divided. PV Sindhu urged meaningful change, Satwik welcomed it for doubles, but Saina Nehwal and coach Vimal Kumar opposed it, fearing it could dilute the sport’s essence.

title: ‘Taken offsite’: Who was the shooter at Trump’s White House dinner? First image emerges
content: President Donald Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots were fired, with the suspect apprehended by the Secret Service. An individual reportedly attempted to enter with a gun but was stopped before reaching the ballroom. Trump praised law enforcement and urged the event to continue.

title: From Ronald Reagan to Trump: How Washington Hilton hotel became scene of panic once again
content: Panic erupted at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Dinner when gunshots were reported, mirroring a similar incident involving President Reagan decades prior. Secret Service agents swiftly escorted President Trump to safety as attendees took cover. The shooter was apprehended, and Trump praised security efforts.

title: How Salim Khan became 'ex-Muslim Salim Wastik'; arrested after 31 years
content: A notorious YouTuber, Salim Wastik, once Salim Khan, has been apprehended by Delhi Police after 26 years on the run. He was convicted for kidnapping and murdering a 13-year-old boy for ransom in 1995. After jumping bail in 2000, he reinvented himself, gaining fame for his controversial online content.

title: IPL: Will MS Dhoni play today against Gujarat Titans in Chepauk?
content: Chennai Super Kings, buoyed by a massive win, face Gujarat Titans with MS Dhoni's return still a mystery. Despite being match-fit, Dhoni is reportedly delaying his comeback to maintain CSK's winning combination. Head coach Stephen Fleming confirmed his progress, while the team looks to leverage home advantage at Chepauk.

title: 'Quite an evening': Trump wanted 'the show to go on' after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
content: Shots were fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner venue, prompting President Trump's temporary evacuation as a security precaution. The shooter was apprehended by law enforcement, who acted swiftly and bravely. Trump confirmed the incident and indicated plans to reschedule the event, emphasizing the safety of all attendees.

title: 'Dear cricket, give me one more chance': Karun Nair's old post resurfaces
content: Karun Nair endured a tough night as Delhi Capitals lost to Punjab Kings, who chased a record 265. Nair dropped Shreyas Iyer twice, allowing him to score 71 and seal the win. The moment went viral, with fans resurfacing Nair’s old “Dear cricket” post. Visibly shaken, the 34-year-old’s struggles highlighted both the cost of missed chances and his ongoing journey of resilience.

title: Trump evacuated after shots fired during White House Correspondents' Dinner
content: Panic erupted at the White House Correspondents' Dinner as shots were fired, forcing President Trump and other dignitaries to be evacuated. A 30-year-old Californian man, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended by the Secret Service after allegedly attempting to breach security with a firearm. An officer was reportedly shot but is expected to recover.

title: Tagged Olive Ridley turtles from Tamil Nadu reach Sri Lanka Dome, reveal migration patterns
content: Considered one of the most productive regions in the Indian Ocean, the Sri Lanka Dome phenomenon typically begins in May, peaks in July, and declines by September

title: Adani Green to invest ₹15,000 crore to add 10 gigawatt battery storage in FY27
content: The proposed addition will be over and above the roughly three GWh of installed storage capacity the company expects to reach shortly, following the commissioning of 1.4 GWh during FY26, it said during its earnings call

title: Zurich-bound Swiss plane aborts take-off at Delhi airport
content: In a detailed statement, an airline spokesperson said there were 228 passengers and four infants on board and six passengers are undergoing medical evaluation

title: Interview | BJP will form govt. on its own strength this time in Bengal, says senior leader Tapas Roy
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee’s one-point agenda is to remain in power, and for that she is willing to do anything, claims former Minister Tapas Roy, now candidate from Maniktala seat in north Kolkata.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Over 50 lakh more people voted this time compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls
content: In the State, people’s interest in polling process has always been higher for Assembly elections when compared to Lok Sabha polls, show data from elections held over the past 20 years; parties carry out a more intensive mobilisation for State polls

title: Mann Ki Baat: PM Modi hails ‘historical milestone’ of Kalpakkam nuclear reactor, strides in wind energy
content: During his Mann ki Baat address, the Prime Minister also urged citizens to take part in the ongoing Census 2027 enumeration process.

title: Panic buying shuts 421 petrol bunks in Andhra Pradesh; CM Naidu orders review
content: Panic buying pushed Andhra Pradesh’s petrol and diesel sales 50% above the daily average on Saturday, shutting 421 of 4,510 outlets

title: Dinesh Trivedi | A man for all seasons
content: A politician with close ties to West Bengal, the BJP leader heads to Dhaka as India’s envoy, with a mandate to reset ties with the neighbour

title: Six border villages in J-K's Kathua brought under Centre's Vibrant Village Programme-II
content: The villages – Bobiya, Kadyala, Gajnal, Karol Krishna, Rathua, and Gujjar Chak — along the International Border were identified at a meeting chaired by Kathua Deputy Commissioner Rajesh Sharma on April 25, says officials

title: Bharat Tex 2026: Apparel leaders urged to promote India as key textile sourcing hub
content: The chairman of Apparel Export Promotion Council emphasised the need to bridge the technology and quality gaps within the textile industry

title: The killing firecracker factories of Sivakasi
content: Deaths due to firecracker unit blasts in the Sivakasi region have become a periodic occurrence, especially during summer. The recent incident, in which 25 workers died in a series of blasts, again spotlights the gross violations in the operation of firecracker factories and the inadequate monitoring of these units

title: Huge rush in Home Guard recruitment examination signals looming unemployment scenario in U.P., says Opposition
content: “We are seeing visuals through social media of long lines outside examination centres, and bus stations are full with the presence of aspirants who are giving the test,” says Ajay Rai, Uttar Pradesh Congress president

title: ED conducts raids in Kolkata in connection with financial fraud linked to fugitive
content: "The searches are part of an ongoing investigation into financial irregularities and suspected links with absconding accused Sona Pappu," says the ED official

title: India, New Zealand to sign FTA on April 27; deal provides Indian exporters 100% duty-free access
content: The FTA between the two countries is one of the fastest-negotiated trade agreements that India has entered into, with negotiations being announced in March 2025 and concluded in December that same year.

title: Mercury likely to touch 44 degrees Celsius in Delhi; IMD issues alert for heatwave-like conditions
content: The weather department has forecast partly cloudy sky with an orange alert for heatwave-like conditions at isolated places by the evening

title: War of words erupts over girl’s death in Ghazipur, U.P.; Rahul Gandhi demands immediate justice
content: Congress General Secretary and Lok Sabha MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra alleged that atrocities against women in U.P. reached its peak under the BJP rule

title: Supreme Court terms U.P. child assault, its aftermath ‘appalling’; calls for independent probe
content: Court calls for immediate SC-monitored and time-bound investigation of the case by a Central independent investigating agency or a Special Investigation Team

title: Ghazipur case: U.P. govt extends financial aid to victim's family
content: Officials also assured the family of every possible support and issued directives to ensure that they receive the benefits of relevant government schemes without delay

title: Hydrologist backs TUDA plan to revive Avilala tank on lines of Hyderabad’s Tank Bund
content: EFI founder Arun Krishnamurthy’s visit comes weeks after TUDA unveiled a plan to develop the 187.27-acre tank on the lines of Hyderabad’s Tank Bund

title: CJI bats for structured national registry of former judges willing to serve in ADR
content: Mediation, Lok Adalats, Arbitration, Conciliation: these are not the back door to justice. For millions of Indians, they are the only door, says Chief Justice of India Surya Kant

title: Head master death case; one arrested
content: “During the investigation, it was found that Pujari was mentally disturbed for a long time and was afraid of being pressured by some people to do wrong things and getting trapped in the payment for the construction work,” says officer

title: From Bengal to Punjab: Amit Shah sets sights on border State’s drug crisis
content: Top sources in the BJP have told The Hindu that Mr. Shah will be visiting the State as often as once a month, through the year and to each district

title: Turnout increases in Tamil Nadu followed spatial patterns
content: While the SIR’s deletions muted turnout in cities like Chennai, northern and semi-urban areas of Tamil nadu registered a good increase in voters compared to 2021 election

title: Effective water quality monitoring mechanism under Jal Jeevan scheme yielding positive outcome, says U.P.
content: The government described it as a remarkable transformation in rural drinking water access under the ambitious Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), converting what was once a pressing public health challenge into a significant developmental milestone

title: Voting underway for local body elections in Gujarat
content: The elections are being held for 15 municipal corporations, including Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Rajkot, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats and 260 taluka panchayats, making it one of the largest electoral exercises in the State.

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: Rumours of rift in Congress over CM nominee in Kerala a ‘media creation’ says IUML
content: Kunhalikutty says if the UDF is voted to power, the Chief Minister would be decided by the Congress keeping in mind the ‘people’s sentiments.’

title: BJP says Kejriwal spent private money on ‘Sheesh Mahal 2’; AAP calls allegation ‘fake’
content: AAP should be called ‘Alishaan Aadmi Party’, says PWD Minister, accuses ex-CM of abandoning simplicity and living in a ‘luxurious manner’; main Opposition party warns of filing a defamation case

title: News in Frames: Back to the roots
content: Far from the influence of urban life, Kurma Gramam in Andhra Pradesh embraces a simple way of living, guided by age-old practices and traditions aimed at sustainability

title: 'Women will respond': Congress slams govt over 'delay' in 33% quota; says implement it at once
content: Congress Gurugram District President Vardhan Yadav said the issue of women's reservation is not merely political, but rather related to social justice and equality

title: The real contest is between the BJP and Congress, not with Trinamool, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Congress leader tries to paint the BJP and Trinamool with the same brush; Mamata-ji did in West Bengal what Narendra Modi has done in the country, he says

title: Census 2027: States asked to identify vulnerable areas, keep biased enumerators out
content: RG&CCI Mritunjay Kumar Narayan asks officials to ensure that vulnerable sections of people, including those affected by communal, political and social tensions are counted as any omission may lead to litigations in future

title: No restriction on women entering mosques, AIMPLB tells Supreme Court
content: Islamic practice does not bar women from entering mosques, though their participation in congregational prayers is not mandatory, says senior advocate M.R. Shamshad

title: U.S. sanctions waiver on Chabahar port ends on April 26, could signal end of 23-year-old connectivity project
content: Officials say they are discussing a possible transfer of IPGL-subsidiary’s stake to local Iranian company temporarily to avoid sanctions

title: UDF will probe “corruption” in the cancellation of DBFOO power supply contracts if it comes to power: V.D. Satheesan
content: Leader of the Opposition blames the LDF govt. for the decision to cancel these contracts, says the move has led to unscheduled loadsheddings and power restrictions across the State this summer

title: Kavitha’s ‘TRS’ may face ECI scrutiny over acronym  similarity
content: In Kavitha’s party’s case, the proposed name “Telangana Rashtra Sena” shares the first two words with the original TRS and carries the identical abbreviation, which the ECI may object to

title: India advises nationals in Mali to stay indoors, exercise caution due to security developments
content: The Indian embassy added that it was closely monitoring the evolving situation in coordination with Malian authorities and will issue further updates as required

title: Experts discuss preventive care and data-led approach to public health challenges
content: The webinar discussed challenges from infectious diseases to NCDs, climate-linked risks, and the growing role of AI and policy in building resilient healthcare systems

title: 'Lawrence of Punjab' docu-series: Congress leader Randhawa gets threat message
content: In the threat message, the names of other persons including Punjab Congress chief Amrinder Singh Raja Warring, SAD leader Bikram Singh Majithia, DGP Gaurav Yadav, Balkaur Singh, father of slain singer Sidhu Moosewala were also mentioned

title: Ashok Kharat case: Oshnology and other deceptions of a godman
content: Ashokkumar Kharat, who police say is a school dropout, allegedly manipulated women and men to build financial assets and political clout over two decades in Nashik. When the Maharashtra police arrested him on charges of rape and fraud, he had come to be known as Captain Kharat, claiming to have worked in the merchant navy in the past. Vinaya Deshpande Pandit speaks to victims, who tell tales of sexual exploitation, financial fraud, and so-called occult practices

title: Madras University invites applications from students for free education scheme
content: Applicants from economically backward strata, orphans, children of widows/widowers, first graduates of family, and transgender persons would be given preference for selection

title: Sculpture by Spanish artist Jaume Plensa unveiled at Kempegowda International Airport in Bengaluru
content: Installed in painted stainless steel and measuring 500 x 319 x 375 cm, the sculpture reflects the city’s diversity, creativity, and global outlook, said a statement from the Foundation.

title: SFI seeks action against ‘harassment’ of medical PG students
content: All 12 PG doctors in the department have lodged a complaint with the Principal, alleging humiliation by two faculty members

title: Officials draw flak over alleged administrative lapses in Kozhikode Corporation
content: Councillors across ruling and Opposition benches demand action against officials over recurring lapses. Mayor says the Corporation will not shield those found guilty and promises strict action.

title: Pinned To Ground: Trump Releases Photo Of White House Press Dinner Shooter
content: The suspected shooter has been identified as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen of Torrance, California, according to reports.

title: "I'm Honoured": Trump Says Assassins Go After "Most Impactful" People
content: Trump, who survived an assassination attempt at an election rally in 2024, added that "I lead a pretty normal life, considering, you know, it's a dangerous life."

title: UP Birthday Party Turns Into Bloodshed: 3 Killed After Cake-smearing Dispute
content: What was meant to be a celebratory gathering at a local gym and later near a residence soured when the victims reportedly applied cake to the face of the birthday boy.

title: She Keeps Losing: Amit Shah After Mamata Banerjee Threatens Case Against Him
content: Amit Shah took potshots at Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee over her statement that she would file a "legal case" against him over "violent remarks".

title: NASA Fellow, Teacher: Cole Thomas Allen Who Fired Shots At Trump Event
content: Unverified social media profiles, including LinkedIn, describe Allen as a mechanical engineer, computer scientist, indie game developer, and part-time teacher based in the Los Angeles area.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran FM Says 'Yet To See If The US Is Truly Serious About Diplomacy'
content: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait -- where 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime -- with no end in sight.

title: Trump Event Shooter Wanted To Do "As Much Harm As He Could": US Attorney
content: Washington, April 26 (IANS) A lone gunman armed with a shotgun, handgun and multiple knives charged a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton before being tackled by law enforcement, officials said, revealing new details about the weapons

title: Severe Heatwave Prevails Across India, Delhi Swelters At 44.5 Degrees Celsius
content: The IMD has forecast severe heat conditions over Delhi-NCR, with maximum temperatures expected to remain significantly above normal over the next few days.

title: Mumbai Nurse Loses Rs 1 Lakh Trying To Buy Rs 299 Dress Online
content: The fraud was orchestrated using a Facebook advertisement, a WhatsApp number and a fake delivery agent.

title: Voting Begins For Gujarat Local Body Polls, Amit Shah To Vote In Ahmedabad
content: Gandhinagar, April 26 (IANS) Voting for Gujarat’s local body elections began on Sunday, with polling underway across municipal corporations, municipalities, district panchayats and taluka panchayats in what is one of the state’s largest grassroots el

title: Video Shows Trump Event Shooter Charging Towards Press Dinner Venue
content: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after a man armed with a shotgun tried to breach security.

title: Trump Event Shooting Live Updates: Trump Says Press Dinner Shooter Was A "Lone Wolf"
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: "Violence Has No Place In Democracy": PM Modi On Shooting At Trump Event
content: PM Modi Reacts To Shooting At Trump's White House Press Dinner Event: "Violence Has No Place In Democracy"

title: Burrata Salad, Then Chaos: Journalists Duck As Shots Fired At US Press Dinner
content: CBS News' White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi heard loud noises and assumed some plates had fallen. Within seconds, Secret Service agents swung over tables to reach and guard the President, First Lady and others at the table.

title: Karoline Leavitt's Uncanny "Shots Will Be Fired" Comment Before Trump Event
content: "He is ready to rumble, I will tell you. This speech tonight will be classic Donald J Trump," White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
